basic_details.job_title: DGPM CBIC Additional Assistant Director Recruitment 2026 - Apply Offline for 172 Posts
basic_details.department: Directorate General of Performance Management (DGPM), Indirect Taxes & Customs, CBIC, Ministry of Finance
basic_details.short_information: Directorate General of Performance Management (DGPM), Indirect Taxes & Customs, Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC) under the Ministry of Finance has issued an official advertisement for DGPM CBIC Additional Assistant Director Recruitment 2026 to fill posts of Additional Assistant Director in various Directorates under CCA, DGPM, CBIC on deputation basis in terms of provisions laid down in Recruitment Rules of Additional Assistant Director dated 31.03.2022 of DGPM.

important_dates.application_start_date: 28 March 2026 (date of publication in Employment News)
important_dates.last_date_to_apply: 12 May 2026 (by 5:00 PM) — within 45 days of publication

qualification.details: Additional Assistant Director: The post is to be filled on deputation basis. The eligibility criteria including educational qualification, experience, and other conditions are detailed in Annexure-C, available on the CBIC website (www.cbic.gov.in) and DGPM website (https://dgpm.gov.in). The Directorate General of Performance Management is the cadre control authority in respect of Group 'B' and 'C' cadres including the post of Additional Assistant Director in various Directorates under CBIC. These Directorates deal with functions such as investigations into cases of evasion of duties/taxes, human resource development, infrastructure, audit, inspections, legal and appellate matters, systems and data management, and data analysis. Candidates whose applications are forwarded by the concerned Ministry/Department/Organization once received in DGPM will not be allowed to withdraw their candidature subsequently.

salary_details.pay_scale: Pay Matrix Level-8: Rs. 47,600–1,51,100; Level-9 (Rs. 53,100–1,67,800) after 4 years of regular service in Level-8
salary_details.details: Selected officers under DGPM CBIC Additional Assistant Director Recruitment 2026 will be placed at Pay Matrix Level-8 (Rs. 47,600–1,51,100) on deputation basis. After completion of four years of regular service in Level-8, officers will be placed at Level-9 (Rs. 53,100–1,67,800) in the pay matrix.

selection_process: The DGPM CBIC Additional Assistant Director Recruitment 2026 is a deputation-based recruitment. Selection will be based on the eligibility criteria as per Recruitment Rules of Additional Assistant Director dated 31.03.2022 of DGPM. The selected officers may be posted to any of the Directorates/Stations throughout India. Applicants may give the option of 05 Directorates in Annexure B(i) and 03 Stations in Annexure B(ii) in order of their preference. However
the options exercised do not guarantee posting at the said Directorate/Station.
how_to_apply: The nomination/application process for DGPM CBIC Additional Assistant Director Recruitment 2026 is through offline/hard copy mode only via the proper channel. Eligible officers must get their nominations forwarded by their concerned CCAs (Chief Controllers of Accounts) along with NOC and all required documents.

Applications/Documents received through e-Office of concerned CCAs will be accepted only where the same are duly received in hard copy. Applications/Documents received only through email will not be accepted.
The nominations of eligible officers along with required documents, duly attested in Hard Copy, must reach by post or special messenger to:
Joint Director (CCA), DGPM Hqrs., 5th Floor, Drum Shaped Building, I.P. Estate, New Delhi – 110002 on or before 12th May 2026 (by 5:00 PM).
Eligible officers for DGPM CBIC Additional Assistant Director Recruitment 2026 must submit their nominations through proper channel well before the closing date.
Step 1 – Check Eligibility: Download and read Annexure-C from www.cbic.gov.in or https://dgpm.gov.in to verify eligibility criteria, educational qualification, and experience requirements.

Step 2 – Prepare Application: Fill in the application/nomination form with personal details, service details, cadre information, posting preference (5 Directorates from Annexure B(i) and 3 Stations from Annexure B(ii)), and all required information.
Step 3 – Obtain NOC: Obtain No Objection Certificate (NOC) from your employer/department before forwarding the application.
Step 4 – Forward Through Proper Channel: Get the nomination forwarded through the concerned CCA (Chief Controller of Accounts) with all required documents duly attested in hard copy.

Step 5 – Ensure Hard Copy Reaches on Time: Ensure the complete application in hard copy reaches Joint Director (CCA), DGPM Hqrs., 5th Floor, Drum Shaped Building, I.P. Estate, New Delhi – 110002 on or before 12 May 2026 by 5:00 PM. Applications received after the last date or incomplete in any respect will not be entertained.

faq.question: Q1. What is DGPM CBIC Additional Assistant Director Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: DGPM CBIC Recruitment 2026 is a deputation-based government job opportunity released by the Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs under the Ministry of Finance. It aims to fill 172 tentative vacancies for Additional Assistant Director posts. These positions are available across multiple Directorates in India.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are announced in DGPM CBIC Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 172 tentative vacancies have been announced for the Additional Assistant Director post. The number of vacancies may vary depending on departmental requirements. These posts are distributed across various DGPM and CBIC Directorates.

faq.question: Q3. What is the last date to apply for DGPM CBIC Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the application is 12 May 2026 by 5:00 PM. Applications must be sent through proper channel in hard copy format. Late or incomplete applications will not be accepted.

faq.question: Q4. What is the eligibility criteria for DGPM CBIC Additional Assistant Director post?
faq.answer: Only serving officers from Group ‘B’ and ‘C’ cadres under CBIC are eligible to apply. Detailed eligibility, experience, and service conditions are provided in Annexure-C of the official notification. Candidates must meet all deputation requirements.

faq.question: Q5. What is the salary for DGPM CBIC Additional Assistant Director Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Selected candidates will be placed in Pay Matrix Level-8 (₹47,600–1,51,100) initially. After four years of service, they may be upgraded to Level-9 (₹53,100–1,67,800). Additional allowances are applicable as per government rules.

faq.question: Q6. What is the mode of application for DGPM CBIC Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The application process is strictly offline through proper channel only. Candidates must submit their nomination through their respective department with all required documents. Applications sent via email will be rejected.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for DGPM CBIC Additional Assistant Director 2026?
faq.answer: The selection is based on deputation rules and eligibility criteria defined by DGPM. There is no direct written exam mentioned in the notification. Selection will depend on service records and departmental approval.

faq.question: Q8. Where will selected candidates be posted under DGPM CBIC Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Selected officers can be posted anywhere in India including New Delhi, Mumbai, Chennai, Kolkata, or Bengaluru. Posting depends on vacancies and administrative requirements. Preferences can be submitted but are not guaranteed.

faq.question: Q9. Is there any application fee for DGPM CBIC Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: No application fee is mentioned in the official notification. Candidates are not required to pay any fee for submitting their application. They should follow only official instructions to avoid fraud.

faq.question: Q10. How to apply for DGPM CBIC Additional Assistant Director Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must fill the application form, obtain NOC, and submit it through their department. The completed hard copy must be sent to DGPM Headquarters before the deadline. Proper documentation and timely submission are essential for consideration.
